programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Jon and Kay plan the next phase of refurbishing an old country pub, while Dannie and Grace decide to build a chicken coop for their rapidly increasing flock.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Sidhbh and Neasa gamble on moving their plastic surgery clinic. Hurricanes wreck David and Gina's holiday flats. And Laura and Lindsay prepare for a new arrival.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, the Rodriguezes help Sarah find a place in a lively location. In Murcia, Paul knows what he wants, but pressure mounts for the Browns when one viewing goes awry.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps recently engaged Michelle and Steve find the perfect family holiday home in and around the beautiful villages of Haute Vienne in southern France, for their £100k budget.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: On Spain's Orihuela Costa, Can Jean Johansson find a holiday home that works for all three generations of Karen and Paul's family, for £75,000?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Ben and Billie dream of transforming their tired orangery into a tranquil retreat. Jon and Carmen rush to finish their wedding path in time for the bride's arrival.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: A look back at one of Kirstie and Phil's biggest builds, when one family went 'all in' to overhaul their Huddersfield home beyond recognition. Paul and Dawn's higgledy-piggledy layout didn't work for them anymore, so Kirstie got the sledgehammer out.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in and around Liverpool, where the first to host is 41-year-old taxi business owner Rob, who plans a Scouse-themed evening.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's night two in Liverpool, and the turn of businesswoman Sam to try and impress her guests with her South-African-themed evening, but her food is a bit hit and miss.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night in Liverpool, gym worker Toria plans to scoop the grand because losing is something that doesn't come easily to her.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Liverpool's fourth host this week is undergraduate Inzy, who is on a mission to prove that students can cook as he eyes the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night of the competition in and around Liverpool, property developer Christine plans to wow her guests with a Spanish holiday themed dinner party.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: In Wales, Paul rides an ultra-modern signal-free line, helps to service a slate-hauling steam train in the birthplace of Thomas the Tank Engine, and experiences the quirky beauty of Portmeirion.

programme.title: Steam Train Diaries
programme.desc: Paul 'Piglet' Middleton journeys through the picturesque North York Moors National Park onboard heritage steam engines. His first adventure: Grosmont to Goathland.

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Novichok attack, ICU medics battle to save the Skripals. The army try to keep the deadly poison from spreading. And a police manhunt begins.

programme.title: The Day the Earth Stood Still
programme.desc: A flying saucer piloted by alien Klaatu, lands in Washington to warn Earth to change its warring ways or risk destruction. Along the way Klaatu finds time to learn more about Earth in the company of Helen Benson and her son.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Belle Starr
programme.desc: Belle Starr is the daughter of a Southern aristocrat. When the invading Yankees take over her land, she vows revenge and heads to Missouri where she marries Confederate outlaw Sam Starr, and joins him and his gang in harrying the occupying troops. In fact, the real Belle Starr was a simple outlaw who, with her Cherokee husband, used the opportunity of the confusion of war to line their own pockets.

programme.desc: Officers contend with riotous World Cup revellers, an offender who has breached his ASBO and a shifty suspected drug dealer. Elsewhere, cops are forced to close the Dartford Crossing in order to halt a stolen vehicle.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard cut through the yellow tape when numerous homeless men are murdered. The case is further complicated by the possibility of a copycat killer.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a petty officer's murder matches the M.O. of a local serial killer, the NCIS team must determine if this is the killer's latest victim or the work of a copycat. Also, McGee and Delilah must learn to compromise before moving in together.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS murder case of a retired Navy Lieutenant Commander forces Ducky to reveal his participation in a secret society that solves cold cases.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo is reunited with his ex-girlfriend, Jeanne Benoit, when insurgents in Sudan attack a group of volunteer doctors, including her husband. DiNozzo and McGee travel to the crime scene for the joint murder and kidnapping case to help the search for the missing doctors.

programme.title: Criminal Minds: Evolution
programme.desc: Alves and Garcia profile who might be targeting Tyler's ex-girlfriend; JJ helps Prentiss work through an existential hangover; when Rossi's Voit delusions reach a breaking point, Rossi confronts Voit and unpacks what has been truly haunting him.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson is called in after a young woman shows up at the hospital, raped and beaten with her head shaved. Haley Kerns refuses to submit to a rape kit, but Benson steals her underwear before leaving the apartment and gives it to Warner to check out. When Benson returns to her apartment, she encounters Star Morrison who admits that Haley is a federal informant working with them on eco-terrorism.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Tutuola pair up to investigate when two teens go missing while their school is on a field trip to a local museum. Their investigation soon turns up evidence that the disappearance was staged to help Janey, who suffers from Turner syndrome, be with her older boyfriend, a man with a predilection for young girls, and to help the boy, Connor, make good on a gambling debt.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives are called in when a young boy is stabbed in the schoolyard, and their investigations lead them to a fellow pupil and his half-sister.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives investigate when a woman is found dead in her apartment, with her son hiding in the wardrobe behind her. Problems ensue when he decides to take revenge on his mother's killer.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: After NCIS captures the hitman known as "The Poet," the team races to uncover who ordered the assassination of a Navy chief.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An actor's foray into experimental drug therapy leads to his murder; Shaw reconnects with a mentor who may hold the key to identifying a suspect.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The University of Glasgow takes on Queen's University Belfast in another first-round match in the quiz competition for students. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The Urban Cyclists and the Country Walkers exercise their brains in this first round battle.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is not looking forward to Onslow's birthday celebrations, but news that a limousine awaits her and Richard to take them to the proceedings soon changes her mind.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is embroiled in his aunt's revenge plot when he tries to live up to his mother's expectations.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama's epic series reaches the 18th century and the birth of modern Britain. Due to an economic explosion, the consumer society is born and agriculture becomes big business. However, many in Scotland are unhappy with the union of the Scottish and English parliaments, and when Bonnie Prince Charlie and his army advance on London, the country's new found peace and prosperity are threatened.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Fabio Capello leads Milan to a new era of glory, while Berlusconi steps onto Italy's political stage, ushering in a turbulent new chapter in the nation's politics.

programme.title: Florence Nightingale: Nursing Pioneer
programme.desc: A look at the life of Florence Nightingale, a trailblazer who revolutionised modern nursing. Born into a rich English family, Nightingale broke through social and gender barriers to pioneer a profession previously reserved for men.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: Jodrell Bank: Tuning Into the Universe
programme.desc: The team explore the remarkable story of Jodrell Bank Observatory and its towering Lovell Telescope, which scientists have used to listen to the cosmos for almost 70 years.

programme.title: Dambusters Declassified
programme.desc: Martin Shaw takes a fresh look at one of the most famous war stories of them all. The actor, himself a pilot, takes to the skies to retrace the route of the 1943 raid by 617 squadron which used bouncing bombs to destroy German dams. He sheds new light on the story as he separates the fact from the myth behind this tale of courage and ingenuity.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Fabio Capello leads Milan to a new era of glory, while Berlusconi steps onto Italy's political stage, ushering in a turbulent new chapter in the nation's politics.

programme.title: Lost Cities of The Ancients
programme.desc: This is the story of the formidable Hittites and their long-lost capital, Hattusha, which has recently been rediscovered. Buried in this lost city is one of the greatest libraries of the ancient world, and all the secrets of this mysterious civilisation were written in two codes - one a unique form of hieroglyphs with which we can recreate the lost world of the Hittites.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Giant warehouses built at the end of our gardens
programme.desc: The team hear from the devastated homeowners who claim the warehouses behind their back gardens were built without proper consultation.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is navigating some severely uneven flooring at an end-of-terrace house in Stoke. Jacqui has layout changes in mind at a flat in east London. A three-bedroom terrace in Kent has Tommy on the hunt for an upstairs toilet.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents a special show on a matter close to her heart - art! In Scotland's capital city Edinburgh, she finds out about a group of famous local artists known as the Scottish colourists and speaks to the grandson of one of them.

programme.desc: The team investigate the work of a scammer pretending to be from Amazon and trying to scam a man in the UK by saying that hackers have access to his online account.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone is hoping the New Forest property market hits all the right notes as he enlists the help of Sonali Shah to find him a family home in the countryside.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in stunning south-west Wales, helping Carol, along with her husband, return to the country where she grew up. With her son Greg supporting her on the house hunt, she's dreaming of a home close to the sea. The property line-up includes a picture-perfect double-fronted house with roses around the door, and a mystery house boasting spectacular mountain views... it's Wales at its most wonderful.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: Dumfries and Galloway - A Book for Lockerbie
programme.desc: The team help commemorate the victims of the Lockerbie bombing, new automata expert Michael tackles a clockwork train, and Kirsten gets hands on with medieval slipware pottery.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family of four Jan, Mick, Shirl and Dave take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.desc: Alistair Appleton faces his toughest assignment to date as he attempts to find broadcasting icon Janet Street-Porter a country retreat, away from prying neighbours.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Two Eurovision enthusiasts need Denise Nurse's help to find them, and their cat Larry, a new home in the Cumbrian countryside. Away from the house hunting, Denise meets up with a young family of keen climbers and outdoor adventurers.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: The team head to Wiltshire to rescue an RAF memorial bench, paper conservator Angie struggles with a challenging Hindu holy book, and Will discovers traditional bee skeps.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter their eighth and final country, Mongolia. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda is thrilled when a hipster coffee shop opens in Soho and offers to promote the café on Senous. Meanwhile, Anne unexpectedly finds fame after a video she's posted goes viral.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: A depressed Richard searches for a real job and embarks on a fitness journey as his 40th birthday looms. Meanwhile, Ken prepares for his first first date in almost 60 years.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This time, he's joined by Julian Clary, Sophie Willan, Harry Pinero, Liza Tarbuck, Judi Love and Tom Allen.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter their eighth and final country, Mongolia. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This time, he's joined by Julian Clary, Sophie Willan, Harry Pinero, Liza Tarbuck, Judi Love and Tom Allen.

programme.title: The Climate Question

programme.desc: The oceans cover about 70% of the planet and are crucial for life on Earth. Oceanographer Helen Czerski reveals how the seas around us regulate the climate and the weather, even in the desert.

programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: In the north east and northern isles, the judges must choose between a reimagined rural house in Aberdeenshire, a converted crofter's house on Shetland and a modern newbuild in Chapleton. Scoring them on architectural merit, distinctive design and personal style, which home will Anna, Banjo and Danny judge worthy enough to go forward to the grand final?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Adam and Lucy race against the clock to produce milk by their contract deadline.

programme.title: Showpeople: Licensed to Thrill
programme.desc: What is a showperson? Run away with the fair and join the family firms who bring the shows to towns and villages across the country. Meet three generations of the Taylor family as they set up in Grangemouth, and find out from finance guru Sarah Sheeran exactly what is involved in buying rides costingt half a million pounds. And meet father-and-son duo Trevor and Kristan Smith as they battle wet weather and family squabbles in Airdrie.

programme.title: Showpeople: Licensed to Thrill
programme.desc: Join the Hiscoe family as they juggle a busy summer season in Ardrossan while looking after two kids and reflect on their decision to move away from the traditional showman lifestyle. The Taylors are in Edinburgh, but this time it's for a very colourful reason. And the Smiths deal with the hardest thing a father and son can go through.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Serie A Scots
programme.desc: Billy Gilmour and Scott McTominay had an incredible first season in Italian football. Their Napoli side won the League and Super Cup, but perhaps their greatest achievement is yet to come with Scotland heading to World Cup 2026. Jonathan Sutherland returns to Napoli to meet the Scottish superstars of Serie A.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve goes in search of ocean acrobats, mischievous coyotes, and deadlies in miniature, but ends up having an encounter with a humpback whale that he will never forget.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall travels across Zambia by motorbike tracking endangered cheetahs.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: With the leaderboard closer than ever, all four teams leave Zaamin National Park and race towards the next checkpoint, Almaty, Kazakhstan's former Soviet-era capital.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip Fitz-Amobi, a brilliant woman, is unsure whether schoolgirl Andie Bell was killed by her lover Sal Singh five years ago. They must see how far they will go to protect Pip from learning the truth if Sal Singh turns out not to be the murderer.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip Fitz-Amobi, a brilliant woman, is unsure whether schoolgirl Andie Bell was killed by her lover Sal Singh five years ago. They must see how far they will go to protect Pip from learning the truth if Sal Singh turns out not to be the murderer.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip Fitz-Amobi, a brilliant woman, is unsure whether schoolgirl Andie Bell was killed by her lover Sal Singh five years ago. They must see how far they will go to protect Pip from learning the truth if Sal Singh turns out not to be the murderer.

programme.title: What's Love Got to Do with It?
programme.desc: Zoe decides to film her neighbour's journey from London to Lahore, where he is to marry the woman of his parents' choice. Zoe experiences a different approach to finding love.

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the world's biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. The world's biggest hip-hop mogul's legacy is now indelibly tarnished.

programme.title: The Instagram Effect
programme.desc: In just ten years, Instagram has gained over a billion users worldwide, shaping culture, trends and the way people live their lives. But its impact on society is increasingly being questioned as governments step up efforts to regulate social media.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy's at rock bottom after the worst year of his life, but with his hand fixed, he's planning a comeback. Tensions mount, and things take an unexpected turn at the press conference.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: In his biggest test yet, Darren Till's threats force John to pull Tommy from the fight. Things take a turn for the better when Tommy shares a New Year's Eve kiss.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian learns about the glittering career of comedian and actor Arthur Askey, as 200 of his personal items go under the hammer.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thrilling new railway adventure following the Silk Road through the most populous country of Central Asia, Uzbekistan.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Rural treasures stolen and livestock lost - police uncover crimes beneath the soil.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists head to the beach to try to find a daughter's lost bracelet, given to her by her loving foster parents. A 160-year-old find leads archive detective Sam on a fascinating trail, which involves the author of Sherlock Holmes, and crafter Sally is inspired by the history of a Brown Betty teapot fished out of the lake - reinventing its story in her modern twist on its tale.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: See Hear explores how technology is transforming life for deaf people, from communication tools and AI to new ways of connecting, working and living in a digital world. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined in the studio by Billy-Jack Gerrard, who is passionate about AI and how it is shaping the future. He explains how AI works, how coding underpins everyday technology and how his own experiences as a deaf coder have influenced his AI career path and thinking.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions as four more contenders take the black chair under the spotlight. The specialist subjects are the history of the Orkney Islands, the films of Laurel and Hardy, Elizabeth David and Sir Garfield Sobers.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Single mum Hayley and her daughter Rosie Bell live in a former council house with virtually no storage space. The team help them to reclaim some empty wall space and find a way to upcycle a piece of furniture that gives them a place to eat together.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Competition for food becomes fierce as lion numbers rise; a lioness risks her life to feed her five cubs; Xudum's newborn leopard cubs face a deadly threat.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At the National Gallery in Ottawa, a desk converted from a piano, Bird scarers and a 16th century emerald jewel.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, experts Charles Hanson and Anita Manning are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Charles is valuing a rare Eureka clock and Anita is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway tour on Tashkent's suburban electric railway, and in Samarkand, witnesses the magnificent architecture of the Registan.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus' mission is to discover the ultimate simple Provençal tomato recipe. After searching for inspiration in the local market, he whips up a simple and delicious Provençal tomato sauce that's traditional, versatile and easy.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers head to Somerset to transform sailors' mess decks at Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. They must turn them into vibrant morale-boosting spaces where the young recruits can cook, eat and socialise. Olympic champion and ex-servicewoman Dame Kelly Holmes joins Michelle to judge the rooms.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: A quiz which compensates for the level of difficulty by awarding points, not just for correct answers but also for `interesting' answers, regardless of whether or not they even have anything to do with the original question asked.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it's flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Growing Up Jewish
programme.desc: Four young people on the cusp of adulthood, all preparing for one of the most important nights of their lives: their Bar or Bat Mitzvah. Dylan, Ayala, Talia and Eve are all about to embark on a uniquely Jewish rite of passage. A boy's Bar Mitzvah, or a girl's Bat Mitzvah, marks their transition to adulthood within the Jewish faith.

programme.title: The Theory of Everything
programme.desc: During the 1960s, Cambridge University student and future physicist Stephen Hawking falls in love with arts student Jane Wilde. Their relationship leads him through personal and scientific challenges.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: See Hear explores how technology is transforming life for deaf people, from communication tools and AI to new ways of connecting, working and living in a digital world. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined in the studio by Billy-Jack Gerrard, who is passionate about AI and how it is shaping the future. He explains how AI works, how coding underpins everyday technology and how his own experiences as a deaf coder have influenced his AI career path and thinking.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the FBI, a quiet Hungarian immigrant landscaper, with no apparent ties to organised crime and no known record, concocts an elaborate extortion plan.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: One patient's rare penis cancer requires a partial amputation, and another patient with severe endometriosis must have a huge cyst and a damaged kidney removed.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Railway Wood is a replica trench in a Kent woodland based on a First World War trench in Belgium. David Harper walks the duckboards with a historian who helped to create it.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway tour on Tashkent's suburban electric railway, and in Samarkand, witnesses the magnificent architecture of the Registan.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning his journey in Osaka, Michael explores the artistic phenomenon of manga, takes a trip on the Shinkansen and, in Nagoya, visits the factory making the smart loo.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his journey at the tense frontier of the Demilitarised Zone before heading to the capital of Seoul, a vast-fast moving city that has become one of the world's most advanced cities.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Working away every week had Dad Simon exhausted and put a strain on his relationship with his wife. She thought Australia could give the family a new lease of life, did a week there prove her right?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A major crack in a two-bedroom cottage in New Malden doesn't stop a fashion student from buying it or from giving the finished project the wow factor. Meanwhile, more seasoned developers in Leeds and Greater Manchester tackle issues in their terraces, with a view to turning a profit on their online auction buys.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another heat of the classic quiz. The specialist subjects are Emily Wilding Davison, The Thick of It, the New Romantics and 20th-century US vice presidents.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Retired couple Steven and Sue love their two-bedroom bungalow, but Steven's mobility issues have left Sue taking on more of the workload, and the tired, cluttered shed is making things difficult. The team need to turn their shed into a practical and welcoming space for Sue to do her volunteer work, and make it easier for Steven to access his mobility scooter.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Lion numbers continue to grow, but there's not enough food to go around; a lioness mum risks her life to feed her five cubs; Xudum's newborn leopard cubs face a deadly threat.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The residents of Whitchurch in Shropshire turn up a treasure trove of items, including a valuable pair of wooden peat buckets, a pair of silver claret jugs crafted by one of the greatest silversmiths of all time, and a collection of snuff boxes and candlesticks valued at £75,000.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Some of the best unseen finds from the show's valuations days from around the country, including visits to the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, Ragely Hall in Warwickshire, the National Maritime Museum Cornwall in Falmouth and the RAF museum in Hendon, London.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure in Uzbekistan continues in Samarkand, one of the oldest cities in Central Asia.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Tywi river valley, from Llansteffan's coastal dunes to fallow deer ruts, ancient oak woodlands, and the remote heights of the Tywi Forest.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah embarks on a journey to investigate her own biological age and whether it can really be controlled. On the island of Okinawa in Japan, she sees the key to some of the healthiest old women in the world.

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: By 2016, Elon Musk is moving fast with several new business ideas: building underground tunnels, creating chips that can be inserted into people's brains and attempting to build the biggest battery factory the world has ever seen.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: A boycott and politics threaten the opening ceremony after a visit from its creative director. Meanwhile, it's time for Ian to think about life after football.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson brings his expertise to bear on the most important stories. In discussion with the BBC's range of experts across the world, he uses their unique local knowledge to go beyond the headlines to get to the unspun truth about the world.

programme.title: Children of the Blitz
programme.desc: During the Blitz, over two million British children were not evacuated, instead staying at home in towns and cities at risk of being bombed. Now, the survivors of the bombings tell their stories.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Garden experts Diarmuid and Carol are joined by guest judge and passionate gardener Patrick Grant to continue the search to find Northern Ireland's Greatest Garden.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Heat three is upon us, which means one thing: six exciting new amateur cooks have left their home kitchens ready to compete for one of four coveted MasterChef aprons.

programme.title: LS Lowry: The Unheard Tapes
programme.desc: An insight into the secretive 'matchstick men' painter as he guides us through his revealing life story in unheard interviews from the end of his life, lip-synced by Ian McKellen.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The quiz heads to Cardiff as Vernon Kay, with Paul Gorton in tow, tests Anneka Rice, Shane Todd and Remi Burgz on their UK knowledge.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Man; The Bros; The Burden; The Mirror
programme.desc: Granny Jojo has brought a boyfriend to her birthday meal and Dad isn't happy about it. Vice Principal Brown gives Gumball and Darwin the ancient school hamster, Chris Morris, to look after for the weekend. Gumball receives a scare-email from a freaky ghost and decides to ignore it, despite Darwin's advice.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Question; The Butterfly; The Lie; The Pizza
programme.desc: After Gumball and Darwin have too much sugary cereal they begin thinking about the mysteries of the universe. In Biology class, Gumball and Darwin are waiting for a butterfly to emerge from its chrysalis. programme.sub-title: The Saint; The Friend; The Safety; The Oracle

programme.title: Blow Your Mind
programme.desc: Dr Chris and Dr Xand continue to find out more from the team of experts and explorers in Greenland. In this episode, they leave their camp high above Store Glacier and head out across the high Arctic seas in the good ship Neptune. They are in search of the amazing sight that is the Petermann iceberg. At a gigantic two billion tonnes of ice, it is the biggest object afloat in the northern ocean.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky's nan has bought him some driving lessons because she thinks he's too old to ride a skateboard. Unfortunately Ricky's not keen and despite the best efforts of his friends and family he won't budge out of neutral. The art is still full-throttle though, with a self-propelling car and a human moped animation, and when they get to the driving centre, Ricky makes epic 3D art with traffic cones that will definitely pass the test.

programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Gooseberry singing a song about everything you have to do to become a Gooseberry Club member.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Pando discover a new climbing frame at the playground. It has climbing bits, tippy bits and even a wobbly bridge. But when Bing gets to the top of the slide it's too scary for him to go down. Bing watches Pando to see how he does it, then he helps Hoppity Voosh to go down. When Hoppity likes it, Bing has a go too.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies say 'eh-oh' to each other in turn and then say 'eh-oh' to flowers, while the children in Tummy Tales make a friendship circle out of hand-prints.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The adventures of a baby monkey and his mum as they face the challenges of daily life together.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: The veggies are playing walkie-talkie hide-and-seek, but Evil Pea steals Carrot's walkie-talkie and uses it to trick the Veggies into getting trapped by the Corn Cobs.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Nicky are sleeping over at Sula's house, but Bing has forgotten Hoppity. The wind causes a branch to make a scratchy noise that terrifies Bing, who isn't very comforted by Sula's fairy rhino. Amma phones Flop, while Sula, Nicky and Bing play 'Nicky tickles' as they wait. Flop arrives, bringing his sleeping bag and Hoppity so they can all sleep over.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: A camping trip with Preston goes very wrong when he won't let them use Mojo's tops. But camping Preston's way soon starts a fire - it's Mojo and Bo to the rescue!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: What's that bouncy, pingy, whacking sound? It's the Penguins playing ping-pong!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels have just got some new walkie-talkies and are having so much fun with them. Duggee calls the Squirrels to come for some blueberry juice, but as Tag is running, he trips over a car and falls through a secret door in the wall. Communicating through his walkie-talkie, Tag doesn't know where he is. He can see lots and lots of things around him, but no-one has any idea where this room is in the house.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo make mud pizzas and deliver them to the grown-ups, but their pedal car's wheel keeps falling off.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Mum are off to Chloe's party and Bingo isn't invited. When a passing cockatoo drops a feather, Bingo has an idea for a new game called `Featherwand', and when she waves her Featherwand she can make objects impossibly heavy.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch makes the Statue of Liberty fall right into the path of a passing ferry.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Lying Fox
programme.desc: When Mr Tod is trapped in Mr McGregor's garden, Peter reluctantly agrees to help him escape, but soon learns that you should never trust a fox.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octopod is attacked by a colossal squid, who drags the ship down an abyssal trench. Captain Barnacles and Kwazii are forced to make a perilous swim outside to confront the gigantic beast.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.sub-title: Now We Are Six to Ten
programme.desc: Are you sitting comfortably? Then we'll begin a bedtime story all about Numberblocks Six to Ten. Learn all about the numbers 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10 with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: TH, I and NG make something spectacular - but what is it? It's marvellous, mechanical and musical and everyone wants one! This episode introduces the 'th-' digraph and the words thing, this, that, thick, thin and them.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: It's a rainy day, and Nick and Norman are bored. The Popcast Planners help write a rainy day song and have lots of fun ideas for Nick and Norman to do outside.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Bath Time
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver sings a song about how much fun bath time is.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella has fun pretending to be a cat, a Bella-Cat. Together with Lift, she visits Tincy the Spider in the SingSong Forest, who sings a spider rhyme, all about taily-waily animals!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing takes his Hoppity Voosh toy to the park to see the ducks and play on the slide. But when he steps in dog poo and heads to Amma's house to get cleaned up he leaves Hoppity behind. Amma helps Bing to focus so that he can remember where he went and retrace his steps to find Hoppity.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'ant', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of ants - a black one, another with two feelers and a teeny-weeny tiny ant!

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu takes the class pet home for the weekend.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou are going to have a feast of their favourite fruit: yum yum baronies. But Lou loses control and eats them all before Kiri can have any!

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: On a rainy day, the Froglets create a shadow show for Bertie and their puppet friends.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos see someone wrapping a present and get carried away trying to wrap things of their own. Back in the Boat, the Stop-Go Car brings them a long scarf that they all get wrapped up in.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Maddie and her friends explore nature at the park, and Mr Tumble is an artist.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: All the Teletubbies are stuck. Tinky Winky's hand is stuck in his bag, Dipsy's hat is stuck, Laa-Laa's ball is stuck and Po is stuck on the Tubby Slidey.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Addi, Bonnie, Ian, Gracie, Connor, Dhilan and their grown-ups. Inside everyone's bags in are gloves which are explored through discovery, play, song and story.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children build and knock down bridges, singing and signing 'London Bridge'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: It is a busy day down in the garden, as Aaron, Adrian and Nelly are looking for some minibeasts! Their journey starts when they lift some logs and stones to see what is underneath, uncovering a family of woodlice scuttling around.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: A paper plate crab is the first make of the day, and an old wooden spoon becomes a spoon monster. The Recycling Express provides yogurt pots to make a palm tree.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad gets embroiled in a game of Hotel, where Bluey and Bingo run a crazy hotel and Dad is the beleaguered guest.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee thought his tie was a little bit plain, so he added some accessories and now it looks amazing. The Squirrels admire Duggee's new look and want to learn how to accessorise themselves. The first step for the Squirrels is to find something they'd like to change. The Squirrels each bring a different item: wellies, a T-shirt, headband, helmet and a potato. They grab sequins, patches, feathers and lights, and then showcase what they have all done.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: The Transport Song
programme.desc: Take a trip with Tino the Artistic Mouse high in the sky or on the ocean.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today, sisters Kyra and Khloe visit a bird centre and meet some friendly owls. With Dad's help they fly an owl and get it to land on a special protective glove they are holding.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kugelis is a recipe from Lithuania that contains bacon and grated potato. Harry has so many potatoes to grate that he has to get his sister to help him! They both work incredibly hard to prepare the dish, which also uses eggs and spring onions. Harry cleverly decorates his table using traditional, beautifully coloured Lithuanian sashes. The kugelis is served with sour cream - let's hope Harry's friends love the taste!

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is looking at things we use when we go to the toilet. She visits a factory to see how hand dryers work. She shows us how infrared light is used to switch the machine on and uses her special camera to see water droplets blasted off her hands in slow-motion. Next, Maddie heads to a toilet roll factory to see the how toilet rolls are made.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Kika and her family are off to the seaside. Since they'll be travelling by bus, she and Dr Amit will need to work together to find the way, using the map on Amit's phone and Kika's super sight. Once she learns a new route, she stores it in her memory bank. She knows 300 routes already - Kika is like a doggy GPS!

programme.title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie Go Wild
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a flock of boisterous, bright green macaws. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet two agoutis. Ultimately, Aneeshwar and Lizzie discover what the wild connection is between these two incredible animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's Waffle's birthday and Mr Willow throws a huge party for him. As the Brooklyn-Bells prepare a surprise for Waffle, Connie and Ruby's dog, Jelly, leads the Wonder Dog astray.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Otto loves dragonflies and is keen for his young friends to see their aerial display. But Baba gets into trouble while they wait for them to begin.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. Ruby sings a diva style ballad about the thing she loves more than anything else, school.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: When Yukee's precious clockwork music box breaks, her garden friends cheer her up with a life-size clockwork performance of their own.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie, an adventurous youngster who happens to be visually impaired, sets out with her best doggy friend Triggs to discover the music and adventure in the sounds of the world around them.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are struggling to build the Clubhouse out of building blocks. It keeps falling over! Duggee explains that the Squirrels need to be a bit of an Architect to create buildings that don't fall down.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three arrives with a bang and a song-and-dance about her favourite number - 1, 2, 3, Everybody Look at Me.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Alphablocks learn to read signs and find themselves playing Alphablock S's super game.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: It's a hot day, so Justin and his friends cool off by visiting an ice cream farm. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble tries to keep Spotty cool but can't seem to control his hose pipe.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po take it in turns to listen to music through a pair of headphones. The Teletubbies love dancing to the music they hear.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how orchards play an important role in the environment. They find out that orchards are mosaic habitats which attract lots of different plants and wildlife and provide shelter and protection for so many creatures. On the way, they spot some fallen fruit that is great for animals to eat and try some fruit that grows in an orchard which they can enjoy too!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Can Evie, Taran and Jaz help Mr Parks rebuild his bug hotel in time for the competition?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all made to go off-road. Grace drives the funny spider car, shows off the daring Formula Off Road buggies and goes for a drive in an eight-wheeled all-terrain vehicle.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: Following Dodge at Pup School, a place full of mishaps and mayhem. Dodge and his pup pals turn school days into adventures with singing, silliness, and occasional squeaky toys.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: George and Freddie pretend to be pirates and head to a nearby boating lake where they hope to find some treasure.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: A honey badger claims a watering hole, so Kwazii challenges her to a digging contest.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Missing Ducklings
programme.desc: A simple matter of duckling-sitting turns into a potential disaster when the ducklings escape into the wood.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie skips into the Clubhouse and greets her friends. As they greet her, they reminisce about when they first met. Arriving with her parents in a long limousine, Norrie, her parents and her 72 siblings flood out of the car. It's just Norrie who is going to stay at the Clubhouse, though, and enjoy some well-needed quiet. She can play alone and hear herself think.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Sunny gets a tummy ache from not washing his hands, so Vida teaches him all about germs and helps his tummy.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea steals Tomato's precious jewel. Supertato ventures to get it back by secretly lifting the jewel from Evil Pea's crown and defeating a crowd of rampaging Mini Peas.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy, an immature and carefree weasel, suddenly becomes the adoptive parent of two ducklings he accidentally hatched.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Tao needs help learning to fly his new wingsuit for a big town event, and Mojo and Bo are the experts. Can they help him find his inner bird before it's too late?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo make mud pizzas and deliver them to the grown-ups, but their pedal car's wheel keeps falling off.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Flutter accidentally clones himself, leaving the berg flooded with scores of badly behaved penguins. As trouble spreads, the Piris must find a way to lose the unruly duplicates.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Mum are off to Chloe's party and Bingo isn't invited. When a passing cockatoo drops a feather, Bingo has an idea for a new game called `Featherwand', and when she waves her Featherwand she can make objects impossibly heavy.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: Penny brings a dinosaur fossil to the nursery, while Bonnie and Hamish plan a secret dino dance. But mysterious footprints have Penny wondering if there's a real dino on the island.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle learns that the summer holidays will soon be over and Evie will be going to school. Determined not to be left alone, Waffle hatches a plan to keep the family at home.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi conjures a unicorn for adventures but realises even heroes need to rest.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Gran's signature Saint Lucian banana bread, they're excited to bake a batch of it. But there's one problem, cake mix has been spilt on the recipe.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah finds an umbrella that doesn't seem to like the rain.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small comes to the rescue when Granny, Major, Mother and the Soup Dragon get into trouble.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa learn that their game of spotting Yummies is, in fact, a hunt for Yummy Dog.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines are eating their Pontipine dinner when Mr Pontipine's moustache flies off! First it gets stuck on the chimney, then the bridge, and then the Gazebo. Eventually, it ends up stuck to the Pinky Ponk's propeller.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Olivia Colman tells the classic tale of the Gruffalo's Child in a Bedtime Story.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane brags about her date at a trendy restaurant, so Sam pretends to be a world-famous heart surgeon to get reservations of his own.

programme.title: Sholay
programme.desc: The tale of one man's fight for justice. In an act of retribution, cold-blooded criminal Gabbar Singh massacres the family of a police officer, Thakur Baldev. Left crippled by his enemy, Thakur is hungry for revenge but, seeing no way of bringing Gabbar to book, he hires two petty crooks, Veeru and Jai.

programme.title: Guru
programme.desc: Following the life of ambitious Gurukanth Desai on his rise to power as a successful business magnate in late 50s India. Through chance and cunning, Guru marries the beautiful Sujata for her dowry, only later to discover he really does love her. Desai becomes a big-time player but his business methods leave much to be desired, and soon newspaper tycoon Gupta, an upright and principled man, is determined to destroy Desai.

programme.title: Dilwale
programme.desc: Set in a vicious and violent environment, Raj and Meera deal with the conflict between their respective families and the repercussions of the merciless violence between them.

programme.title: Pankh
programme.desc: Film highlighting a phenomenon peculiar and exclusive to the Indian film industry, the practise of casting children in roles opposite to their natural genders.

programme.desc: Brad and Barney kickstart this leg of their tour with a bang - in honour of the famous Swede, Alfred Nobel. Barney treats his dad to an infamous fishy delicacy for breakfast, then takes him paintballing - with a Western twist! Before leaving the country, Barney treats ABBA mega fan Brad to the ultimate musical surprise.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Soap Opera Party

programme.sub-title: The One With the Fertility Test

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler consider their options. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang heads to Barbados for a palaeontology convention where Ross is the keynote speaker. Rachel harbours steamy thoughts about Joey while David prepares to propose to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey & Rachel Kiss
programme.desc: While the friends are still in Barbados, Joey tries to tell Ross about his feelings for Rachel and Ross wants to confess to kissing Joey's girlfriend Charlie, but it is easier said than done. Meanwhile, Monica, Chandler and Phoebe can hear their amorous conversations through the hotel's paper-thin walls. Also, Monica's hair problems go from bad to worse, and Phoebe has some bad news for Mike's girlfriend on her birthday.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine
programme.desc: Ross drinks too much wine while dining with Rachel, Joey and Charlie. Elsewhere, Frank Jr cannot cope with his precocious triplets and pays Phoebe a visit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Joey nervously anticipate their first real date, Monica and Phoebe try to avoid an obnoxious friend with a fake British accent, and Ross emerges from a tanning booth with a two-toned appearance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gets Monica and Chandler to delay their long-awaited trip to Vermont for Emma's first birthday bash, but problems ensue when Emma falls asleep and a racy cake turns up.

programme.title: The Longest Yard
programme.desc: Paul `Wrecking' Crewe was a revered football superstar back in his day, but that time has since faded. When a messy drunk driving incident lands him in jail, Paul finds he was specifically requested by Warden Hazen, a duplicitous prison official well aware of Paul's athletic skills. Paul has been assigned the task of assembling a team of convicts, to square off in a big football game against the sadistic guards.

programme.title: Jack Whitehall Gets Around: Live from Wembley Arena

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Boob Job
programme.desc: Monica secretly asks Joey for a loan because she and Chandler are strapped for cash, Phoebe invites her boyfriend to move in with her, and Rachel's apartment baby-proofing drives Joey crazy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Memorial Service
programme.desc: When Chandler jokingly posts an outrageous biography about Ross on Ross' college alumni website, Ross retaliates in kind, sparking an internet war. Meanwhile, Monica gets extremely vigilant after a weak-willed Phoebe asks for help to stop her from contacting Mike after their relationship ends.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Hoping to win a huge jackpot, the friends pool their money and buy dozens of lottery tickets, but they argue over how to spend the fortune if they win, and Monica irritates everyone else by buying tickets just for her and Chandler. Exasperated, Phoebe takes the bowl of everyone's tickets and threatens to throw it off the balcony. The others make up, but a pigeon startles Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A visit to the set of a soap opera inspires Rachel to dream of a romantic encounter with Joey, and Chandler must take Ross on a weekend getaway after Monica is unable to take time off from work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Soap Opera Party
programme.desc: Ross is excited when he meets a stunning palaeontology professor, but when he takes her to Joey's rooftop party for the actor's soap-opera co-stars, she intimidates him with her impressive list of former boyfriends. Meanwhile, Rachel and an intoxicated Monica make fools of themselves at the party chasing after the hunky daytime actors.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fertility Test
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler have an embarrassing encounter with an old acquaintance at a fertility clinic, Ross plays Cyrano to help Joey woo a professor, and Rachel sneaks into a spa for a massage hoping Phoebe won't find out.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler consider their options. When Chandler invites a handsome colleague round for dinner, Monica and Chandler embarrassingly interview him as a potential sperm donor. Meanwhile, while shopping with Phoebe, Rachel admits that she still has a thing for Joey.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang heads to Barbados for a palaeontology convention where Ross is the keynote speaker. Rachel harbours steamy thoughts about Joey while David prepares to propose to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey & Rachel Kiss
programme.desc: While the friends are still in Barbados, Joey tries to tell Ross about his feelings for Rachel and Ross wants to confess to kissing Joey's girlfriend Charlie, but it is easier said than done. Meanwhile, Monica, Chandler and Phoebe can hear their amorous conversations through the hotel's paper-thin walls. Also, Monica's hair problems go from bad to worse, and Phoebe has some bad news for Mike's girlfriend on her birthday.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine
programme.desc: Ross drinks too much wine while dining with Rachel, Joey and Charlie. Elsewhere, Frank Jr cannot cope with his precocious triplets and pays Phoebe a visit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Joey nervously anticipate their first real date, Monica and Phoebe try to avoid an obnoxious friend with a fake British accent, and Ross emerges from a tanning booth with a two-toned appearance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gets Monica and Chandler to delay their long-awaited trip to Vermont for Emma's first birthday bash, but problems ensue when Emma falls asleep and a racy cake turns up.

programme.title: The Longest Yard
programme.desc: Paul `Wrecking' Crewe was a revered football superstar back in his day, but that time has since faded. When a messy drunk driving incident lands him in jail, Paul finds he was specifically requested by Warden Hazen, a duplicitous prison official well aware of Paul's athletic skills. Paul has been assigned the task of assembling a team of convicts, to square off in a big football game against the sadistic guards.

programme.sub-title: Produce Power from Waste, Customize Supercars, Make Super Steel Swords

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, Callum and Alison put together a fun-filled menu, despite Callum's not-so-cheery nature. And there's an outburst over the Essex dialect during dessert at Mike and Carol's.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Pool Party
programme.desc: Emma and Patrick are childhood sweethearts, but lack of funds means that Emma has been waiting nearly 10 years to get Patrick up the aisle. However, there's a catch - they have both agreed in the presence of a lawyer that Patrick must organise the wedding alone.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Prague Stag
programme.desc: Ryan and Fern are head over heels in love. Ryan tries to pull off a Hollywood wedding to dazzle his bride, and has to remember that he's marrying his bride and not his best man.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits Café Hon in Hampden, Maryland, Chiarella's in Philadelphia, and Leone's in Montclair, New Jersey, to see whether the restaurant owners have taken his advice on board.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon head to the historic Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, where he meets an owner so stubborn and set in his ways that he doesn't realise he's the reason his restaurant is losing money. Owner Tom bought the business for his daughter Andrea, but six years later he is still refusing to let her run it. Gordon must fight a controlling boss who doesn't react well to his criticism.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.sub-title: A Home That Doesn't Measure Up
programme.desc: Lisa and Chris bought their Nashville home six years ago, but with four kids and Grandma all squeezed under one roof, it's time to overhaul their log-cabin interiors so they can afford a bigger nest. Jonathan gears up for a radical renovation.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, Omer and his wife Adeeba, sustainable food growers Bobby and Alison, and Liz and Stuart compete in a week featuring experimental food, a compost loo and a stately home.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The comics retrieve pineapples from a pond and inventively repurpose umbrellas. And in a fitting climax, Frankie Boyle goes to town on a gigantic cat scratching post.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will's parents have just divorced and he has unwillingly had to move area and change schools. He was previously at a private school, so has inherited some snobbish tendencies.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Tired of their monster commute, Jinny and Raymond want to trade their huge suburban heritage home for a turnkey property closer to the city. They call in the Property Brothers to help make it happen.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Northampton, Pam and Glenn's Spanish food gets a grilling from their guests, Victoria and Delroy serve up Caribbean cuisine, and Wendy and Mark's night doesn't go to plan...

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Midlands, image-conscious couple Sarah and Jack kick off the competition with a clean-living menu. Day two sees married couple Liz and Martin cook an Italian feast inspired by recipes from Liz's Italian mother.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Hannah and Andy have been together for three years and soon moved in with each other, but it is spoilt princess Hannah who rules the roost. So, will Andy be able to plan her Disney fairytale wedding?

programme.sub-title: The One with the Oompa Loompas

programme.title: The Virginian
programme.desc: A ranch foreman falls out with an old friend over a woman and is then involved in hunting him down when he turns to crime. programme.title: The Black Shield of Falworth
programme.desc: Myles Falworth, the son of a disgraced knight, is determined to prove himself. With the help of the Earl of Mackworth and despite the sneers of his contemporaries, he trains for his knighthood and finally earns his spurs and the nation's thanks when he foils a plot to de-throne King Henry IV. His love interest is Lady Anne Mackworth, while the Earl of Alban is the requisite baddie that Falworth must overcome.

programme.title: Criss Cross
programme.desc: An armoured car guard must join a robbery after being caught with his ex wife by her gangster husband.

programme.title: Twelve O'Clock High
programme.desc: "We're in a war, a shooting war. We've got to fight, and some of us have got to die". Frank Savage is the WWII commander drawing every ounce of fight from his weary airmen.

programme.title: Jerry & Marge Go Large
programme.desc: Jerry Selbee discovers a mathematical loophole in the Massachusetts lottery, and with the help of his wife, Marge, he wins millions of dollars.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: Journalist Eddie Brock is trying to take down Carlton Drake, the notorious and brilliant founder of the Life Foundation. While investigating one of Drake's experiments, Eddie's body merges with the alien Venom -- leaving him with superhuman strength and power. Twisted, dark and fueled by rage, Venom tries to control the new and dangerous abilities that Eddie finds so intoxicating.

programme.title: T2 Trainspotting
programme.desc: Mark Renton reunites with old friends Spud, Sick Boy and Begbie after returning to the only place that he can ever call home.

programme.title: Ravenous
programme.desc: Chilling hybrid of black humour and gore set in the US during the 19th century as a stranger arrives at a remote military outpost, claiming to be the sole survivor of a group who became stranded and resorted to cannibalism for survival. Setting out to investigate his claims, the soldiers discover that the truth is even more disturbing than the wanderer's story.

programme.title: The Virginian
programme.desc: A ranch foreman falls out with an old friend over a woman and is then involved in hunting him down when he turns to crime. The Virginian wins the battle for the affections of Molly Wood, and Steve responds to losing in love by aligning himself with a gang of cattle rustlers. The Virginian looks on while a posse catch Steve and hang him, and guilt-ridden, he swears to go after the gang-leader.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel goes fishing with an old friend and makes an alfresco fish dish by the river. Plus, he makes watercress soup, a winter salad, and roast apples.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: Nigel proves that the food we buy week in week out can always be exciting as he introduces us to some new delicious flavour combinations to reinvigorate the weekly shop. Recipes include ricotta beefburger, sesame seed salmon with mirin and lemon and thyme cake.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: Nigel cooks seven delicious dishes, with simple recipes that make quality ingredients go further, proving we can treat ourselves without breaking the bank. Including chicken and leek pie, mushroom medley and raspberry and cinnamon meringue.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's a ranch-style pizza revolution with four fabulous new ideas. Ree is cooking mini cauliflower pizzas for lunch with her daughter, then there's a meal kit for a mash-up of two favourites in an awesome herbed chicken Caesar salad pizza.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond's sharing the fundamentals of care packages while putting one together for her college kid, Alex. She's packing up an utterly delicious lemon and raspberry pound cake and wrapping up snickerdoodles in the best DIY way.

programme.title: Jamie's Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver concludes his journey in the west of Scotland. Jamie finds that haggis is being given a West Indian twist in Glasgow's oldest Scottish restaurant. Later, Jamie brings his adventure to a close with an ambitious feast combining the best of local game with an Italian recipe, seared venison loin with Scottish risotto and golden pheasant hash.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Tex-Mex Entertaining
programme.desc: Former food store owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Cook With a Pro
programme.desc: Ina gets a cooking masterclass with three amazing professional chefs; pastry chef Natasha Pickowicz, Parisian chef Marie-Aude Rose and chef Missy Robbins.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers have their knowledge of pastry tested to the max with a savoury take on a French classic, a multi-layered Moroccan Technical, and a towering Showstopper.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary shows us how to cook dishes to impress guests at a dinner party without getting hot and bothered in the kitchen, including two delicious canapés, a salmon and asparagus starter, a main course of guinea fowl and a decadent chocolate tart.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. They're enjoying the luxury trip of a lifetime aboard The Royal Scotsman. James visits the kitchen aboard the train, where he cooks up a traditional duck a l'orange. Nick visits a special bakery housed in an old prisoner of war camp and James uses the bread to make a tasty ham and cheese toastie in the grounds of historic Scone Palace. The day ends back aboard the Royal Scotsman, where James and Nick dine in style in the beautiful dining room.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a trip to the stunning Cairngorms National Park, where he's joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. Amongst their adventures, they visit some friendly reindeer and brave the rapids on the River Spey, where they go whitewater rafting. James takes a quad bike through the ancient pine forests, where he meets up with a farmer and his special breed of Highland cattle, which then inspires him to cook a tasty Highland beef carpaccio.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy tastes ribs in the Bronx, a chorizo with salmon in Washington state, and pinto beans fried in fatback in South Carolina.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy enjoys lobster rolls, philly cheesesteaks, and a meal covered in french fries.

programme.desc: Adam tries a monster Burritozilla and then competes in a super-spicy challenge, the Special Number Two, where he must finish a fiery ramen soup that even the chef won't try.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam bites into a chicken and cheddar biscuit sandwich, and then takes on the Ludicrous Wings Challenge, where he has 15 minutes to finish a dozen jumbo wings soaked in super spicy sauce.

programme.desc: The team make a hero sandwich cake for a local deli's 100th anniversary party. It is also Easter week, so the team is swamped with cranking out all of the holiday favourites.
