programme.desc: Peppa and family are watching TV... until it breaks! While Grampy Rabbit fixes it, the family get a new TV which can do everything... but it doesn't beat their old TV.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A storm cancels Peppa and her family's adventure plans so they decide to take it inside. Using their imagination, they go sledding, climb a mountain and have a campfire in the attic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are playing in their pool. They help the garden animals cool down but when it rains, the animals are happy and so is Peppa. Rain makes muddy puddles for jumping in!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are in Granny and Grandpa Pig's Garden. Granny's friends are playing folk music! They play music for Grandpa Pig and then Grandpa Pig makes apple juice for everyone!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is bedtime and Peppa calls Kylie Kangaroo and is surprised to learn that it is not morning or winter where she is! Daddy Pig explains this using a big ball to represent the Earth.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While visiting Suzy and Emily's house, Peppa is surprised that there are extra rules which she does not have in her own house. On the drive home, Peppa explains to Mummy Pig that different houses have different rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig is confirming she is having a new baby. Mummy and Daddy Pig share the exciting news with Peppa and her brother, George.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are on the search for a new home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family renovate their home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: With the building work complete, it's time to decorate! Peppa and George have fun choosing how to decorate their new attic bedrooms. It's dinosaurs for George, but what will Peppa pick?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa's family car breaks down. While it is being repaired, they borrow a shiny new one from Grandad Dog's garage.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig are taking Peppa and George to a festival - a jigsaw festival! Peppa is amazed by all of the completed jigsaws around the festival.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle is making a wicker basket. She explains it is her hobby - something you do to relax. The children discuss hobbies and learn about each other's favourite things to do.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle teaches the children how to play the recorder. They practise a lot at home, then put on a performance for all the parents to enjoy.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.desc: A new synthesiser arrives for Daddy Pig, and his family collect sounds for it from around the house while he is out at work.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: During a picnic in her garden, cousin Chloe shows Peppa and George fun things to do with flowers.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Rebecca and Richard Rabbit are playing a marble run game at Peppa's house. But when Daddy Pig joins in and improvises tunnels and bridges, the marble rolls out of the bedroom, causing chaos and nearly knocking Goldie out of her bowl.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When George loses the metal wind-up key for his toy robot in the long grass, Grandpa Pig cannot find his metal detector to help George track it down.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: For World Book Day, the playgroup must dress up as a character from their favourite book. But Peppa can't decide which as she has so many favourites.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George, Mummy and Daddy all go on a lovely trip to the children's festival. There is lots to see and do, including singing, making crafts and listening to stories.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Day two at the festival and it has rained all night. The festival is very muddy and Peppa is thrilled. Luckily, it rains every year and Miss Rabbit is prepared with lots of muddy activities.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: A Bug's Strife; All the Rage
programme.desc: Dad tears the house apart trying to find the cricket ruining his peaceful day alone in the house; tired of getting pummeled during dodgeball, Lincoln and the gang use Clyde's untapped rage without him knowing.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: The Krusty Bucket; Squid's on a Bus
programme.desc: Plankton creates a clone of himself and Mr Krabs, but neither restaurant is safe from the newcomer. Squidward gleefully switches jobs with a bus driver for an easy ride until SpongeBob and Patrick climb aboard.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob volunteers to babysit, then SpongeBob and Patrick volunteer as guards at the museum, while Squidward attempts to hang his painting unnoticed.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin talks Dave into buying a used tour bus, Dave has no idea of the problems ahead. Alvin signs the Chipmunks up for a game show that forces losing contestants to perform dangerous stunts.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin accuses Dave of yelling at him too much, a therapist moves in to help Dave control his anger. When the school digs up an old high school time capsule, Miss Smith and Dave work together to keep an old secret.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob breaks his precious alarm clock. Then Karen quickly learns that kids grow up too fast after her new child arrives in the mail.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: Back Out There; Spell It Out
programme.desc: Clyde, worried that Lincoln is hung up on Ronnie Anne, tries to cure his post-breakup blues. Lucy turns to a spell book to teach her siblings a lesson.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Clyde, desperate to learn how to act normal around Lori, enlists Leni's help. But when Lori sees them together, her imagination runs wild.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin encourages Simon to write an email, venting about his professor. When the email accidentally gets sent, they must stop the teacher from reading it. When Simon notices that Theodore has a hard time saying no, he creates the No-Buddy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the Principal's cat goes missing, Theodore gives her a music box. When Dave discovers his Grandma's music box is missing, he calls the police. Theodore's been mistakenly mailed off in a box. Now Simon and Alvin have to get him home.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob's favourite jellyfishing net goes missing in `The Pink Purloiner', and then in `Squid Wood', SpongeBob decides that if he can't hang around with the real Squidward, he'll make his own.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: The Best Day Ever; The Gift of Gum
programme.desc: SpongeBob is going to have the best day ever - but his day does not go as planned. Patrick gives SpongeBob a giant ball of used gum on Best Friend's Day. SpongeBob tries to get rid of it, but fails at every attempt.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Lynn finds an unexpected TV sports-watching buddy in the family's next-door neighbor, Mr. Grouse; when Lisa realizes that Pop Pop won't be around forever, she turns to science to ensure his longevity.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Clyde arranges for an exchange student to stay in 'Exchange of Heart', then in 'Community Disservice', Lola takes a new classmate under her wing and gives her a social transformation.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin sneaks into Simon's new virtual reality game and is transported to a world in need of a hero; the chipmunks discover Officer Dangus has a twin brother that's a bully and encourage Dangus to stand up to him.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Last Days Of Summer / The Littlest Robot
programme.desc: Alvin wastes his entire summer playing a video game then tries to do all the fun things he'd planned in one day; Simon invents a robot with emotions and an ability to learn.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick tell all sorts of fibs to sell chocolate door to door in `Chocolate with Nuts', and then in `Superfriends', Mermaidman and Barnacle Boy are back once more.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Patrick comes to boating school with SpongeBob, and soon gets on his nerves in `New Student Starfish', and then in `Clams', Mr Krabs takes SpongeBob and Squidward clam fishing.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: It is prehistoric Bikini Bottom, and we spend a day in the life of SpongeGar, Patar, and Squog. Our three Neanderthal friends discover fire, only to put it out.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: Roadie to Nowhere; A Fridge Too Far
programme.desc: Luna worries that her ambition to be a rocker is unrealistic. When food Lincoln saved in the fridge gets taken, a territory war in the fridge ensues.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: Selfie Improvement; No Place Like Homeschool
programme.desc: When Carol Pingrey's photo gets more likes on social media than Lori's she tries to one-up her. Lola is being temporarily homeschooled. Thinking it looks like fun, the other kids try to get in on it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Alvin's Wild Weekend/For Whom the Bell Tolls
programme.desc: Alvin begs Dave to spend a weekend alone with him. Dave agrees and gets sucked into a series of wild Alvin adventures. A hypnotist accidentally turns Simon into a bumbling fool before he's scheduled to participate in an academic contest.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin sneaks into Simon's lab to look at his latest invention, only to accidentally clone himself. With two Alvins, there's double the trouble. Alvin tells a small lie that quickly turns into a huge disaster, dragging everyone into his tangled web.

programme.desc: Last year Hanson released a Christmas album of their own! So we invited Isaac, Taylor and Zac to choose ten of their all-time festive favourites.

programme.title: Duncan James' Eurovision Top 25

programme.title: Eurovision Party Night!
programme.desc: Eurodance, queens of soul and masters of rave collide in the biggest, baddest, funkiest party known to man. This will have you dancing.

programme.desc: Trying to impress someone with your cool moves? You could try these! 60 music videos from the '80s with some great, but also some very suspect, dance moves and routines!

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Weathered: Earth's Extremes
programme.desc: Heat in Portland, Ore.; exploring why some urban areas are hotter than others in Medellin, Colombia; a visit to Phoenix -- the hottest city in the U.S.

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: Brighton's Royal Pavilion was built in 1787 by George IV while Prince Regent. He took a small beach house and turned it into a flamboyant, multi-domed, Indo-Saracenic wonder palace.

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: In the second of this three-part series, Professor Robert Bartlett explores the impact of the Norman Conquest of Britain and Ireland. Bartlett shows how William the Conqueror imposed a new aristocracy, savagely cut down opposition and built scores of castles and cathedrals to intimidate and control. He also commissioned the Domesday Book, the greatest national survey of England that had ever been attempted.

programme.title: The Wheelchair President
programme.desc: David Reynolds re-examines the war leadership of American president Franklin Roosevelt. At the height of war, Roosevelt inspired millions with stirring visions of a new and better post-war world, but it was a world he probably knew he would never see. He was commander-in-chief of the greatest military power the world had known, and yet a man whose paralysis from polio made him powerless to accomplish even the most minor physical tasks.

programme.title: Higgins Ridge
programme.desc: The incredible story of 20 elite firefighters (smokejumpers) who parachuted into the wilderness to put out a wildfire burning on a mountain ridge.

programme.title: John Legend - Live at the iTunes Festival
programme.desc: John Legend is an artist with intellectual skills, he studied English with a major in African American literature at the University of Pennsylvania. In college, he participated in a jazz group called Counterparts and at the time was introduced to singer Lauryn Hill, playing piano on the track 'Everything Is Everything' from the singer's first solo album.

programme.title: The Moody Blues - Days of Future Passed Live
programme.desc: Celebrating the 50th anniversary of their landmark album, Days of Future Passed, The Moody Blues took to the road to perform the entire LP live. An electric evening at The Sony Centre, Toronto, where the band was joined on stage by a full orchestra for the first time ever, is the definitive performance of this seminal album.

programme.title: Rodrigo y Gabriela - Live @ Garorock Festival 2014
programme.desc: The Mexican duet takes the stage at Garorock Festival, in July 2014.

programme.title: Seal - Live at Soundstage
programme.desc: Seal's one-of-a-kind soaring, husky baritone voice is perfect for any genre; he's delighted fans and earned critical acclaim while staying true to classic, honest songwriting in his remarkable two-decade career.

programme.title: James Taylor - Austin City Limits

programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion are reunited in a classic car. This coast to coast antique odyssey sees them kicking off in the seaside town of Morecombe. Charles flexes his muscles, and his wallet, picking up a 250 year old table. Christina takes a heavy horse to the Sheffield auction, in spite of some conservative spending.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team are called out to people's homes with a mission to sift through a lifetime of possessions and find forgotten treasures that can be sold at auction.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they make the finest classical guitars, how do they produce fast tractor mowing machines and how do climbers keep the blades turning on a wind turbine?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they build life rafts that can hold 150 people, how can mind control techniques help golfers improve their shots, and how do they produce millions of celebratory stamps?

programme.title: Body Cam

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Following a team of experts as they investigate whether evidence exists that could challenge the conviction of a man in prison for a murder he denies. Every year, over 500 people in the UK are convicted of murder. A guilty verdict can only be challenged if new evidence comes to light. Inside Justice is a specialist charity led by Louise Shorter, supported by a panel of criminal lawyers, ex-police officers and forensics experts who offer assistance to prisoners who are protesting their innocence.

programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla that's been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed holiday to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: The Meg
programme.desc: A massive creature attacks a deep-sea submersible, leaving it disabled and trapping the crew at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean. With time running out, rescue diver Jonas Taylor must save the crew and the ocean itself from an unimaginable threat: a 75-foot-long prehistoric shark known as the megalodon.

programme.title: Morbius
programme.desc: Biochemist Michael Morbius tries to cure himself of a rare blood disease, but when his experiment goes wrong, he inadvertently infects himself with a form of vampirism instead.

programme.title: Troy
programme.desc: When Trojan prince Paris woos Helen, Queen of Sparta, away from her husband Menelaus, the legendary warrior Achilles is called to lead the armies of Greece against the city fortress of Troy.

programme.title: Wonder Woman
programme.desc: Diana, princess of the Amazons, is raised to be an unconquerable warrior on a sheltered island paradise. She discovers her full powers and true destiny after leaving home to join the fight to end all wars.

programme.title: Green Lantern
programme.desc: A test pilot is granted a mystical green ring that bestows him with otherworldly powers, as well as membership into an intergalactic squadron tasked with keeping peace within the universe. Based on DC Comics characters.

programme.title: Gremlins
programme.desc: A placid small town is brought to its knees by an infestation of destructive small creatures with a vicious sense of humour.

programme.title: Spanglish

When Flor and her daughter Cristina are invited on holiday with the family, all the relationships involved take a new turn.

programme.title: The Ballad of Wallis Island
programme.desc: Old tensions resurface when former bandmates who were former lovers reunite for a private show at the island home of an eccentric millionaire.

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle
programme.desc: A recently engaged woman becomes obsessed with meeting the grieving widower she heard on a late night radio show. Sam, the widower, receives hundreds of letters following the radio phone-in which was instigated by his son Jonah.

programme.title: Little Fockers
programme.desc: Greg and Pam have been together for ten years, and now they're the proud parents of two adorable twins. However, money is tight and in order to keep his family afloat, devoted nurse Greg takes a second job working for a drug company. That development doesn't sit well with Jack, whose previous suspicions regarding his hapless son-in-law soon come back in full force after learning of this development.

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle
programme.desc: Guided by a rebellious and charismatic coach, British ski jumper Michael "Eddie" Edwards trains for the 1988 Winter Olympics in Calgary, Alberta.

programme.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
programme.desc: Andie Anderson writes an advice column for a fashionable magazine and agrees to demonstrate how easy it is to attract then alienate a man in just ten days. She chooses Benjamin Barry, little knowing he's an advertising executive whose job rests on proving he understands women so well that he can make one fall for him in two weeks.

programme.title: The Hangover
programme.desc: A crazy Las Vegas bachelor party turns into a race against time when three hung-over groomsmen awaken after a night of drunken debauchery to find that the groom has gone missing, and attempt to get him to the alter in time for his wedding.

programme.title: The Hangover Part II
programme.desc: A group of friends go to Thailand for a quiet and restrained stag party, but are dismayed to wake up in a mysterious hotel room, with a man missing and no memory of the night before. Of course, Alan manages to get himself in with the crowd.

programme.title: The Hangover Part III
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Doug take Alan on a road trip to help him through a personal crisis.

programme.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
programme.desc: Knockabout comedy set in the sexist world of 1970s newscasting. Ron Burgundy, San Diego's top-rated anchorman, reigns supreme in the all-male world of television news, until the arrival of an ambitious female reporter upsets the status quo. When she steps in to present the programme in Ron's absence, an uncivil war breaks out in the newsroom.

programme.title: Drift

programme.title: Turtles All the Way Down
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old struggling with obsessive-compulsive disorder reconnects with her childhood crush, she faces the potential of finding love and happiness despite her mental condition.

programme.desc: Based on a true story, this film, set during World War II, tells the story of the all-female baseball league that was started while the American men were away fighting in Europe. After a team of potential players has been recruited, former player Jimmy Dugan coaches the girls and prepares them for the World Series.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Dunkirk
programme.desc: Germany advances into France, trapping Allied troops on the beaches of Dunkirk. Under air and ground cover from British and French forces, troops are slowly and methodically evacuated from the beach using every vessel that can be found.

programme.title: About a Boy
programme.desc: Parallel coming-of-age stories of a layabout `serial nice guy' living a posh, carefree lifestyle off his deceased father's fortune, and a bright but awkward youth who's tired of his mother's depressed, boyfriend-less state. Their paths collide when the boy fabricates a two-year-old son and joins a group called S.P.A.T. (Single Parents Alone Together).

programme.title: Turtles All the Way Down
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old struggling with obsessive-compulsive disorder reconnects with her childhood crush, she faces the potential of finding love and happiness despite her mental condition.

programme.title: Super-Charlie
programme.desc: A young boy attempts to solve an ongoing series of crimes with the help of his super-powered baby brother.

programme.title: Bookworm
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Mildred's world is turned upside down when her estranged father, a washed-up magician, comes back to look after her and takes her camping to find the mythological Canterbury Panther.

programme.title: Rebellious
programme.desc: A headstrong princess is stolen by an evil sorcerer, and her scholarly fiancé must use his wits in an epic quest to find her while she boldly challenges her captors and plots a daring escape.

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: The Emoji Movie
programme.desc: Three emojis embark on an epic adventure through a smartphone to save their world from being deleted.

programme.title: Ghostbusters: Afterlife
programme.desc: When a single mother and her two children move to a new town, they soon discover that they have a connection to the original Ghostbusters and their grandfather's secret legacy.

programme.title: Jumanji
programme.desc: Alan Parrish disappeared as a young boy. Nobody believes his friend's story that he was sucked into the game they were playing. Twenty-six years later, two children find the game in their attic, and when they start to play, Alan is released, but the game isn't over yet, and Alan must finish before he is really free.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: The Boss Baby
programme.desc: A wildly imaginative seven-year-old boy discovers that his new brother, Boss Baby, is actually a spy on a secret mission, and only he can help thwart a dastardly plot that involves an epic battle between puppies and babies.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Mouse Hunt
programme.desc: Slapstick tale which follows the misfortunes of two hard-up brothers whose plans to renovate and sell a valuable building are jeopardised by a rogue mouse. The brothers soon find themselves pulling out all the stops in order to evict their tiny resident.

programme.title: Harriet the Spy
programme.desc: A precocious child who hopes to be a writer lands in hot water with her classmates after her secret notebook is stolen.

programme.title: The Tiger Rising
programme.desc: When a boy discovers a caged tiger in the woods near his home, his imagination runs wild as his life begins to change in unexpected ways.

programme.title: The Tiger Rising
programme.desc: When a boy discovers a caged tiger in the woods near his home, his imagination runs wild as his life begins to change in unexpected ways.

programme.title: The Ogglies
programme.desc: Young Max meets the Ogglies, a family of green, garbage-eating creatures who live at the dump in the town of Smelliville. When a contractor plans to build a wellness temple at the dump, Max and his pal must hatch a plan to save the Ogglies' home.

programme.title: Gloria
programme.desc: The mob eliminates Jack Dawn and his family. His six year old son, Phil, escapes with Gloria, the girlfriend of one of the gangsters, but they are chased because of a book containing mop accounts.

programme.title: ...And Justice for All
programme.desc: Satirical drama in which an idealistic lawyer who tries to win cases for clients trapped in a hypocritical and corrupt legal system finds himself defending a venal and self-righteous judge accused of rape. The judge wrongly assumes the lawyer's ideals and disdain for the courts will drive him to get an acquittal, despite the evidence and the loathing they have for each other.

programme.title: Psycho
programme.desc: Classic chiller about the grisly goings-on in a remote motel run by a timid and decidedly odd young man and his domineering mother. The film totally changed Hollywood's perception of the horror genre and broke many of the movie industry's taboos.

programme.title: Anatomy of a Murder
programme.desc: Frederick Manion, an army lieutenant, is arrested for the murder of bartender, Barney Quill. In defence, he claims that the victim had beaten and raped his wife. With such evidence somewhat lacking, Manion's lawyer, Paul Biegler attempts to save his defendant from prosecution, setting his assistants in search of witnesses.

programme.title: Enter the Dragon
programme.desc: A kung fu expert is assigned to penetrate an island fortress to destroy an opium and white slavery empire.

programme.title: Jaws III
programme.desc: Second sequel to Steven Spielberg's 1970s blockbuster transports the action from Amity Island to a new undersea kingdom attraction in a Florida theme park. The capture of a baby great white shark is big news and brings crowds to the park - until its mother arrives on the scene.

programme.title: Birdy
programme.desc: A soldier returns from Vietnam to find his boyhood friend, also a fellow soldier, in a military hospital, having suffered a mental breakdown in which he has become detached from reality and imagines that he is a bird.

programme.title: Escape to Victory
programme.desc: When ex-German international and camp commandant Van Steiner suggests a match between the POWs and the Germans, it swiftly escalates into a propaganda exercise.

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: A disturbed undercover cop recovering from his wife's death is teamed up with a detective with a stable family background to crack an underground drug ring. The line between duty and off-duty begins to blur, however, when the daughter of one of the cops is kidnapped by the gun-toting heavies.

programme.title: Scarface
programme.desc: A Cuban immigrant from Castro's jails cuts a violent path of destruction on his way to the top of Miami's drug trade.

programme.title: Against All Odds
programme.desc: A fading professional football player falls in love with the girlfriend of a small-time criminal he's been hired to find.

programme.title: True Romance
programme.desc: Shop clerk Clarence falls for a prostitute, steals a fortune in cocaine and goes on the run, pursued by psychotic mobster Vincenzo.

programme.title: Reef Wrecks
programme.desc: The waters near the Florida Keys are home to as many as 1,000 shipwrecks.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: Despite its small size, Swaziland boasts big five status and some 132 species of mammals have been recorded there. Giraffe, hippo, zebra, and a variety of antelope can be found across the country, as can predators such as jackals and hyenas.

programme.title: Whale with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve returns home to the Atlantic waters of the British Isles as he considers the question of what humanity can do to ensure the enduring future of whales, which have already survived so much.

programme.title: Island of the Monsoon
programme.desc: In Sri Lanka's parched lowlands, monkeys eat succulent water lilies at a lake in a forest clearing. Alongside them, deer and wild boar come to drink. It's a peaceful scene - but it can't last.

programme.title: Amazing Animal Friends
programme.desc: Meet more Amazing Animal Friends including Charlotte, a schnauzer who enjoys bath time with her guinea pig pal Wallace, and a randy ram called Hamish who likes to sneak off to live with a herd of dairy cows instead of his own kind.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: The Lilayi Elephant Nursery near Lusaka in Zambia is home to four orphan elephants. The two oldest have been there since they were just a few months old. These boisterous babies are out-growing the nursery and it is time to move to big school.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: An immense, dry land dominates southwestern Africa. Here, sand covers the land in rippling dunes and rain drains away as quickly as it falls. Kalahari, meaning place of great thirst, is an appropriate name for one of southern Africa's greatest wildernesses.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: Kinda baboons have turned their backs on the aggressive displays of dominance usually associated with these large monkeys and lead altogether more peaceful lives.

programme.title: Volcanic Odysseys
programme.desc: In Indonesia we find regions where the air is so toxic that it's 40 times above human safe levels and sulphuric acid vents from the ground in dense yellowish clouds. Elsewhere, small volcanoes are so active, they are said to erupt every 15 minutes.

programme.title: Secrets of the Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Wading into wetlands, where trees and water meet to nurture new life. Diving into an underwater forest that protects young fur seals, visiting a mangrove sanctuary where a curious band of aquatic misfits hides out, and more.

programme.title: Surviving the Amazon
programme.desc: Swing through the canopies of the Amazon's trees with a look at its 1,200 species and the creatures who call their leaves, branches and shadows home.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Animals go to extraordinary lengths to survive in the hottest and driest extremes, from the Sahara Desert to Australia.

programme.title: Tales From Zambia
programme.desc: Along the bank of the Luangwa River is a troop of eighty or so baboons. This is the story of a family, and a young male baboon who faces difficult choices as he comes of age.

programme.title: Reef Wrecks
programme.desc: The Bahamas is known as the underwater movie capital of the world. Hollywood has been coming here for decades, making epic movies and dramatic TV shows in its crystal clear waters.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Oxford to create a beautiful but productive kitchen garden for inspirational Soup Kitchen founder Icolyn Smith. Despite being 88 years old, this caring hero still cooks and serves the vulnerable and homeless twice a week, every week. The team share clever design tricks that make the most of long narrow gardens; give tips on space saving ways to grow apples; and suggest ways of furnishing your outdoor spaces without breaking the bank.

programme.desc: Safety Bay Beach in Western Australia has been hit by gale force winds and a vessel named Unhinged has been torn from its anchorage and washed ashore. programme.desc: Cec Drury, the butler at the Colonists' Club, has just closed up for the night when a body appears out of nowhere from above, crashing onto the roof of the car in front of him. So begins one of Dr Blake's most unlikely and remarkable cases.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty goes to a quiet fishing village to meet her long-lost cousin Kate.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Everyone's favourite Essex girls have an explosive time in Chigwell, with upheavals for Sharon, Tracey and Dorien.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is concerned about Nora's unusual behaviour and decides the only course of action is to follow her and her new lodger.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo thinks he's made a killing when he remembers a shilling he's still owed from his schooldays.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Howard finds an incriminating piece of jewellery on his person he unwisely asks Clegg for help.

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: A tailor arrives to measure up Captain Geering and Colonel von Strohm, and an explosive bottle of gin is doing the rounds and arrives in the cafe. Edith gives it to her mother - for medicinal purposes.

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is captured by German troops while on a sabotage mission and imprisoned by General von Klinkerhoffen, who orders General von Strohm to make his punishment severe.

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Following his supposed execution, Rene finds himself having to arrange his own funeral in order to satisfy Herr Flick, who hopes to use the occasion for a spot of Resistance-spotting.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The UCOS team reinvestigates the murder of an unnamed vagrant strangled on a tube train 15 years ago when DNA tests on a suspect in a warehouse robbery reveal him to be the dead man's son.

programme.title: Dalziel and Pascoe
programme.desc: Andy and Peter investigate a murder case when a skeleton is unearthed in a Yorkshire mining town. As they begin to unravel a tale of theft, adultery and betrayal, the killer strikes again.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty goes to a quiet fishing village to meet her long-lost cousin Kate.

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: Spade arrives in Bozouls; a brief trip becomes a new chapter as he falls for a local vineyard owner; years later, Spade remains, a widower and retiree quietly living out his golden years in peace, but the return of his adversary changes everything.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Crystal Skulls
programme.desc: Mysterious crystal skulls could be long-lost relics of Aztec and Maya civilizations that grant supernatural powers. Experts investigate new leads to reveal if these strange objects are genuine artefacts or if they're modern-day fakes.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Ancient pyramids are among the greatest wonders of the world and exist across many civilisations, explore the origins of these structures as experts question if they were independently constructed or perhaps the design of something more mysterious.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Nazi Belief in the Occult
programme.desc: It was believed that part of the Nazi party was involved and influenced in all manner of occult beliefs from their uniforms to their huge rallies.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Cutting-edge investigations at the Bermuda Triangle might confirm the newest theories of what really goes on in this deadly stretch of water.

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: December, 1648.The Puritan government has banned all celebrations and King Charles I is imprisoned in Windsor Castle. During Christmastide, the monarch would ordinarily feast and celebrate but instead he spends Christmas Day alone.

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Tuesday 9th January 1649, crowds gather in Cheapside London as a proclamation is read out. King Charles I will be put on public trial at Westminster Hall in ten days' time; it sends shockwaves through the City.

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: On the 23rd of January 1649, the third day of King Charles' trial, Charles continues to publicly dispute the High Court's legitimacy. There is no choice other than to move forward and enforce the charge against him, with the help of witnesses. After two days of hearing witness testimonies concerning the King's presence in battle, the evidence against him is overwhelming.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The Tunnels of Gibraltar were a Second World War espionage base, hidden in the Rock of Gibraltar, which lay undiscovered for decades. At Atlas-1 Test Site, are the remains of an ingenious US Airforce project that continues to keep people safe.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: Britain's history is recorded in it's monuments but now the landmarks are crumbling. Teams of people across the country are keeping our heritage standing, these are our landmark fixers.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: In the Scottish capital of Edinburgh, hidden from view, a series of vaults tell a murky story of opportunity, crime and destitution. At Fort Schwatka Unalaska are the remains of an Alaskan battleground, where a foreign invader set foot on American soil for the first time since 1812.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An Appalachian dream that conjured the spirit of the old west, left to rot on a North Carolina mountainside. In the ex-Soviet republic of Georgia are the remains of an ingenious sky-high solution to a ground-based problem, Chiatura, once known as the Venice of the air.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring Agdam, a city in Azerbaijan raised to the ground in a conflict decided by a new weapon of war.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explores an industrial ruin in the Florida Everglades in the south of the United States that once competed in the greatest race humankind has ever witnessed: the mission to land on the Moon.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring traces of a mass deception hidden at Ponte Dirillo in Italy, as well as a once lavish theatre in Ohio and an ancient city with a poisonous secret.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The historic Royal Albert Hall is in need of urgent repairs, however it is a difficult job. A specialist team is required to restore the building to the highest order before its grand reopening.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: Salt water and iron don't mix and Southend Pier, built back in 1888, has seen better days. Now a team of specialists are working hard to prevent it falling into the sea.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A collection of Austin Mini Metros arrive for auction. Also going under the hammer is a baby Fiat 850 coupe and a haulier offloads part of his vintage commercial vehicles collection.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has three scooters to collect, while also finding room for a Daimler and a teardrop caravan.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Veterinarian Noel Fitzpatrick is one of England's top animal doctors and the subject of 2010 documentary programme `The Bionic Vet', which showcased some of the work done at his Surrey clinic. On `The Supervet' Fitzpatrick and his staff treat some of the country's hardest-to-cure pets with cutting-edge treatments and surgery. The programme tells the emotional stories of the pets, their owners and the passionate team that push the boundaries of veterinary medicine to save the animals from life-threatening conditions.

programme.sub-title: Coco, Sapphire and the Cheese Chis

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Trevor McDonald ventures inside two jails that hold some of America's most notorious women criminals, Indiana Women's Prison and the state's Rockville Correctional Facility.

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Following Trevor McDonald as he ventures inside two jails that hold some of America's most notorious women criminals - Indiana Women's Prison and the state's Rockville Correctional Facility.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey and her gang challenge the Viney family to sort their lives out by decluttering in just seven days. Not only did grandma of six Sue fill her house up with her late mum's belongings, she also acquired the contents of a recently closed gift shop. Daughters Nicki and Donna want to spend more time at the house with their kids, but all the clutter means there isn't any space.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of 3 December 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. The next morning, the car was found abandoned, balanced precariously on the edge of a quarry.

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha Christie's later life, and discovers how, amid the turbulent social and political change of the 1930s and 40s, newfound personal happiness ushered in a golden age for her writing.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Bangers team can't believe their luck when a 1970's family-favourite, a Volvo 144, rolls through Mathewson with just 15,000 miles on the clock.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team fall for a Subaru Pick-up, which has all the Mathewsons in agreement. But will it all come crashing down when picking up parts proves tricky?

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear about the effect of World War I on the domestic front.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Rob Bell looks at how the greatest invasion in human history was being prepared in Southern England and the buildings that helped with D-Day and Operation Overlord.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team of expert scrutinisers inspect a valuable ceramic model and a rare nativity painting.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: To mark the centenary of construction commencing on the Titanic, Fiona Bruce and the team set up for business in the former drawing offices of Harland and Wolff in Belfast, where the ship was conceived.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There is a triple collection of desirable cars in store for Derek when an MG Midget, an Austin-Healey frogeye Sprite and an RS2000 come up for auction.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack is heading out to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference. And luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what has been brought in for auction.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear about the effect of World War I on the domestic front.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Rob Bell looks at how the greatest invasion in human history was being prepared in Southern England and the buildings that helped with D-Day and Operation Overlord.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Asia's jungles are beautiful and exceptionally diverse, but whilst they provide shelter, food and opportunity, they are also full of dangers and hidden threats.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: In the planet's most densely populated continent, animals live at extremely close quarters with people, from tigers making a home in town to elephants stopping traffic for food.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave is on HQ duties and admires a Punto that's two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: It's a battle of little and large as the team get creative with a 1996 Fiat 126 and then run their own consumer test on a 2004 Bentley Continental GT.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: From the bone-dry deserts of the Atacama, where penguins weave their way through a minefield of snapping sea lions, to the lush cloud forests of the Andes, where Andean bears scale 30-metre trees in search of elusive fruits, South America is full of the unusual and ingenious.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Isolated for millions of years, the weird and wonderful animals marooned here are like nowhere else on Earth. In the north of this island continent is the Daintree, one of the world's oldest tropical forests.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory hunt for neglected cars they can fix and flip for a profit. Any losses incurred by their flipping come out of their own pockets.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a child's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Off the coast of Yorkshire, Siwrengale is heading out for the beginning of the North Sea whelk season. Skipper Darren Warters is on the hunt with sons Regan and Lewis. Asian demand has created a lucrative fishery in this small patch of the North Sea, but winter storms have badly damaged their pots. Miles of rope must be untangled before they can begin fishing in earnest. South east of Shetland is 'Long Hole' - the deepest waters in the North Sea.

programme.desc: Britain's history is recorded in it's monuments but now the landmarks are crumbling. Teams of people across the country are keeping our heritage standing, these are our landmark fixers.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The team build a Protection Deck; more then 300km of scaffolding cover the whole bridge, encasing it in an exoskeleton of constantly changing steel.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1973 Ford Escort Mexico is the team's most expensive purchase to date. To save some cash Derek helps on their second buy - a 1968 Riley Kestrel.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in Thornton-le-Dale.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Illuminating statistics help to show how, after 1916, the imperial powers redoubled their efforts to win the war.

programme.title: Churchill's Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: Following an astonishing story which challenges our understanding of the Second World War and our perception of world-renowned leaders.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for another busy day at the Titanic Drawing Offices in Belfast. Objects uncovered include a medicine chest from early Victorian times, complete with many intact medicines, a historic document marking the end of World War II, and a pair of rare Irish plate buckets worth the price of a new car.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team travel to the Bishop's Palace, Wells in Somerset. Among the objects under scrutiny are one of the oldest objects ever seen on the Roadshow, a painting by Rolf Harris's grandfather, and a plate reputedly found in Captain Scott's tent on the ill-fated Antarctic expedition.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970 Excalibur Roadstar, once owned by Roy Orbison, arrives at Thornton le Dale and Derek heads to County Durham to collect a 1935 Austin 7 Ulster that's been completely rebuilt.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic British classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room; Dave heads to North Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8 E that's had an alternative paint job.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Illuminating statistics help to show how, after 1916, the imperial powers redoubled their efforts to win the war.

programme.title: Churchill's Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: Following an astonishing story which challenges our understanding of the Second World War and our perception of world-renowned leaders.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: In the heart of Asia lie vast arid lands. These deserts and dry grasslands are so hostile that they push life to the limit. The animals that live here are some of the earth's toughest.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Many passionate people are dedicated to saving the wild animals of Asia. These individuals and their ingenious solutions give us hope and optimism for the future of the continent.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A fully restored Class 47 diesel engine is transported from its home at Crewe Heritage Centre in Cheshire to the Ecclesbourne Valley Railway in Derbyshire.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: 300,000 square miles of ocean. Three GPS-tracked voyages, filmed around the clock. One week at sea. Crabber Tydus has had a terrible year, missing months of fishing due to breakdown. With the boat finally fixed, skipper Stiggy pushes the crew to work around the clock to fill the tank with ten tonnes of crab. After covering expenses, Stiggy gambles and moves his pots further south.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: High above the city of Gibraltar, Barbary macaques, Europe's only non-human primates, live lives full of kidnappings and high drama, whilst in the cemeteries of Vienna `grave robbing' European hamsters do battle with each other for food.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: More than any other continent, North America is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change. For animals that live here this poses great challenges, but for those with a pioneering spirit it can also offer great rewards.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory hunt for neglected cars they can fix and flip for a profit. Any losses incurred by their flipping come out of their own pockets.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions. At the fairground, the boys pick up a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Scalloper Golden Promise is pushing force-6 winds to get to newly opening scallops beds ahead of his rivals. With the boat first to the grounds, they are in for a big pay day - but only once they cover the £8,000 fuel expenses to get here. Family-owned trawler Adenia leaves Lerwick aiming to land 1,000 tonnes of mackerel in Norway in just three days' time.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.
